description: Bilingual French-Arabic food content showcasing European cuisine offerings with pricing, menu items, and restaurant location tags to drive F&B discovery and dining interest.
structural_recipe: Post high-quality food photography featuring French or European dishes. Use dual-language captions (Arabic primary, French secondary with transliteration). Include: specific dish names in both languages, Saudi Riyal pricing, star ratings or recommendation phrases ('انصح فية' - I recommend), restaurant name and location tags (@rest.riyadh format), emoji accents (🇫🇷🍽✨), and hashtag-style organization with bullet points for menu items.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences value international cuisine as aspirational lifestyle content. Bilingual presentation signals sophistication and European authenticity. Transparent pricing builds trust. Recommendation language ('I advise') leverages social proof and personal endorsement which drives high consideration for dining decisions in premium F&B segment.
transplantation_caveats: French language usage may limit reach outside cosmopolitan urban areas; consider audience literacy levels
Premium pricing (75-85 SAR range) assumes affluent target demographic; may alienate budget-conscious segments
Restaurant tag dependency requires existing establishment relationships and location-based discoverability
cultural_constraints: Ensure food items comply with halal requirements and Islamic dietary guidelines
French cultural references should not conflict with Saudi traditional values
Alcohol should never be suggested or visible in imagery
